den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
due: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umiray Dumaget Agta.
duet: noun. A musical composition in two parts, each performed by a single voice (singer, instrument or univoce ensemble).
dun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dusun Deyah.
dune: noun. A ridge or hill of sand piled up by currents of wind or water.
dunt: noun. A stroke; a dull-sounding blow.
end: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ende.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
ess: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Siberian Yupik.
ness: noun. A promontory; a cape or headland. (Frequently used as a suffix in placenames.)
nest: noun. A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
nude: adjective. Without clothing or other covering of the skin; without clothing on the genitals or female nipples.
nudest: adjective. superlative form of nude: most nude
nus: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nuer.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
nutted: verb. simple past and past participle of nut
sen: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nanerige.
send: verb. To make something (such as an object or message) go from one place to another (or to someone).
sends: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of send
sent: verb. simple past and past participle of send
set: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sentani.
sett: noun. The system of tunnels that is the home of a badger.
sned: noun. A cut, a cutting; a slash, a slight wound; a lopping or pruning.
sneds: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sned
stet: noun. A symbol used by proofreaders and typesetters to indicate that a word or phrase that was crossed out or changed should remain as it
stets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of stet
stud: noun. A male animal, especially a stud horse (stallion), kept for breeding.
student: noun. A person who studies or learns about a particular subject.
stun: verb. To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.
stunt: noun. A daring or dangerous feat, often involving the display of gymnastic skills.
stunted: verb. simple past and past participle of stunt
suds: noun. Lather; foam or froth formed by mixing soap and water.
sue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Suena.
sued: verb. simple past and past participle of sue
sues: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sue
suet: noun. The fatty tissue that surrounds and protects the kidneys; that of sheep and cattle is used in cooking and in making tallow.
sun: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Sundanese.
sunset: noun. The moment each evening when the sun disappears below the western horizon.
ted: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tepo Krumen.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
tends: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tend
tent: noun. A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
tun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tunica.
tuned: verb. simple past and past participle of tune
tut: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Altaic languages.
tuts: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tut
unset: adjective. Not set; not fixed or appointed.
unsets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of unset
used: verb. simple past and past participle of use
des: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Desano.
duett: noun. Archaic form of duet (“musical performance by two people”).
ned: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nde-Gbite.
sds: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tunisian Berber.
sed: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sedang.
ssu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Susuami.
sten: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Sten
stent: noun. A slender tube inserted into a blood vessel, a ureter or the oesophagus in order to provide support and to prevent disease-induced
sud: noun. A bubble of lather or foam (the singular of suds).
suent: adjective. Alternative spelling of suant (“even; smooth”)
sus: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Susu.
tdt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tetun Dili.
tendu: noun. A move in which the leg and foot stretch to point in a particular direction, but the foot does not leave the floor.
tes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tengger.
tnt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tontemboan.
tss: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Taiwan Sign Language.
tsun: noun. Dated form of cun (“Chinese unit of length”).
ute: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ute.
